questions.text: If A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {4, 5}, then the number of elements in A × B is:
questions.explanation: A × B (Cartesian product) has n(A) × n(B) = 3 × 2 = 6 elements. The elements are (1,4), (1,5), (2,4), (2,5), (3,4), (3,5).

questions.text: A function is a relation in which every element of the domain has a unique image.
questions.explanation: A function is a special type of relation where each element of the domain has exactly one image in the codomain.
The function f(x) = 1/x is undefined at x = 0. So the domain is all real numbers except 0.

questions.text: If f(x) = x^2 and g(x) = x + 1, find (f + g)(x).
